objective.primary_goal: Implement a deterministic indexing system for three JSON source files that enables stable exact addressing for RFC-6902 patch workflows without making indexes part of source-file SSOT.
outcome_definition: JSON Pointer is the canonical exact locator
mutable arrays are addressed through compiled semantic-ID to JSON Pointer maps
generated index artifacts are deterministic and external to source JSON
Plan CLI normalize and compile flows own index verification and generation
validation gates fail closed on identity, pointer, and staleness violations

included_workstreams: schema changes required for stable identities
excluded_workstreams: inline navigation index as source-file authority
fine-grained instruction-document indexing without structural refactor

rules: All authoritative patch paths SHALL resolve through JSON Pointer.
JSONPath MAY be used for discovery or search but SHALL NOT be the authoritative locator.
Relative JSON Pointer MAY be used only for narrow internal validator or schema-local logic.
Byte-level indexing SHALL NOT be implemented for this plan.
Schema fragments MAY be used only when referencing subschemas, not as the main runtime locator.

array_safety_mechanism.description: Mutable arrays SHALL be addressed by semantic identity through generated maps rather than hardcoded numeric positions.

rules: Indexes SHALL NOT be hand-maintained inline inside the three source JSON files.
Indexes SHALL be regenerated from source JSON.
Indexes SHALL NOT become editing authority.
Source JSON remains authoritative content SSOT.
Generated index JSON becomes authoritative locator artifact for tooling only.
No tolerance for duplicate semantic identifiers in index-eligible arrays.
No tolerance for missing required identity fields in index-eligible arrays.
No raw array-position patching for indexed collections.

prerequisite_blockers.title: Duplicate gate identity in technical specification gate_registry
prerequisite_blockers.problem: Duplicate GATE-008 breaks uniqueness and prevents reliable semantic ID lookup.
required_resolution: Update all internal references to renamed ids.
completion_criteria: All references to gate ids consistent.

prerequisite_blockers.title: Ordinal numeric identifiers in template final summary enhancements
prerequisite_blockers.problem: Numeric ids are ordinal and unstable, so they are not valid semantic identities for indexing.
required_resolution: Replace numeric or ordinal enhancement ids with stable semantic ids.
Define id pattern for enhancement entries.
Fail execution if any enhancement entry still uses ordinal-only identity.
completion_criteria: Each enhancement has a stable semantic id.
No enhancement identity changes when array order changes.

prerequisite_blockers.title: execution_phases lacks stable element identity model
prerequisite_blockers.problem: Plain-string or identity-free execution phase entries cannot support reliable element-by-identity indexing.
required_resolution: Decide whether execution_phases remains structural or is redesigned as index-eligible objects.
If structural, declare replace-whole-array semantics and do not index by element.
If index-eligible, refactor entries into objects with stable phase ids and schema validation.
Fail execution if classified index-eligible without declared stable identity.

prerequisite_blockers.title: System layer components lack stable component_id
prerequisite_blockers.problem: Using component name as identity is fragile and insufficient for deterministic element-level indexing.
required_resolution: Add stable component_id to each component object if component arrays are to be index-eligible.
Define component_id pattern and uniqueness rules.
Fail execution if component arrays are classified index-eligible without component_id.
completion_criteria: All index-eligible component entries contain unique component_id values.
No component array relies on display name as sole identity.

contract_rules: No array may be treated as index-eligible by implication.
Every array in scope SHALL have an explicit classification record.
Index-eligible arrays SHALL declare identity_field, identity_type, identity_pattern, patch_mode, index_output, duplicate_identity_action, and missing_identity_action.
Structural arrays SHALL declare replace-whole-array semantics and SHALL NOT emit ID-based index output.
Empty arrays expected to become identity-patched later SHALL declare identity schema before first population.

decision_test.question: Will tooling patch individual elements here?
Is the patch target selected by business identity rather than list position?
Does every element already contain a stable identity field?
Is the identity semantic rather than ordinal?
Can the array reorder without changing meaning?

notes: No hardcoded numeric array positions in consumer patches for index-eligible arrays.

known_index_targets.condition: verify actual final field name and semantic stability before implementation

notes: Arrays such as gate_registry and components require identity remediation before indexing.
execution_phases requires explicit classification before implementation.

special_handling: Normalize and parse section_purpose_map.sections[].template_path as template-facing JSON Pointer fragment references.
Validate consistency between any referenced template path and actual template structure.

NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_INSTRUCTION_DOCUMENT.current_limitation: Single markdown content blob blocks reliable subsection-level machine addressing.

required_decision.reason: Fine-grained machine indexing is required for robust CLI-targeted section updates.

interim_policy: Until structural refactor is complete, permit only coarse-grained addressing of /document_metadata and /content.
Do not generate fine-grained section index entries from markdown heading parsing heuristics.

maps_description: by_section for named top-level or section identities
additional maps only when backed by declared array-classification metadata

determinism_rules: Stable key ordering in emitted JSON.
No nondeterministic UUIDs in artifact body.
Identical source content must produce identical artifact bytes.
source_sha256 must reflect canonicalized source bytes used by generator.

implementation_phases.title: Define indexing contract
tasks: Create schemas/navigation_index.schema.json.
Create machine-readable array classification registry for all arrays in scope.
Record canonical locator policy, JSONPath secondary policy, and fail-closed rules.
Record prohibition on raw array-position patching for index-eligible collections.
acceptance_checks: Every array in scope has explicit classification.
Contract schema validates.
Canonical locator and derived-artifact rules present.

implementation_phases.title: Resolve prerequisite blockers
tasks: Resolve BLK-001 duplicate gate ids.
Resolve BLK-002 ordinal enhancement ids.
Resolve BLK-003 execution_phases classification and schema.
Resolve BLK-004 component_id gaps.
acceptance_checks: All blockers closed.
No duplicate semantic ids remain in index-eligible arrays.
All candidate index arrays have valid identity schema or are explicitly structural.

implementation_phases.title: Inventory addressable structures
tasks: Enumerate direct-pointer-safe nodes.
Enumerate index-eligible arrays and their identity fields.
Enumerate structural arrays and their replace-whole-array semantics.
Enumerate instruction-document coarse-only paths until refactor is complete.
Persist machine-readable inventory.
acceptance_checks: Inventory covers all target files.
Inventory entries align with contract registry.
No unclassified array remains.

implementation_phases.title: Refactor instruction document for fine-grained indexing
tasks: Replace monolithic markdown content blob with structured JSON sections.
Assign stable section keys.
Update schema and any consumers.
Backfill migration logic if legacy content must be preserved.
acceptance_checks: Instruction subsections addressable by stable JSON Pointer paths.
No reliance on markdown heading parsing heuristics for authoritative addressing.

implementation_phases.title: Implement index generator
tasks: Create scripts/build_json_navigation_index.py.
Parse source JSON deterministically.
Compute direct pointers for object-keyed nodes.
Scan index-eligible arrays and emit semantic-id to pointer maps.
Reject missing or duplicate semantic ids.
Emit deterministic sorted JSON output.
acceptance_checks: Generator output validates against navigation index schema.
Repeated runs on unchanged input yield identical bytes.
Generator fails closed on duplicate or missing identities.

implementation_phases.title: Implement locator resolver
tasks: Create scripts/resolve_json_locator.py.
Resolve semantic ids to current JSON Pointer paths.
Support file-scoped queries such as gate id, step id, phase id, and section.
Return machine-readable success or fail-closed errors.
acceptance_checks: Resolver returns exact current pointer for every valid indexed identity.
Resolver rejects unknown identities.
Resolver never fabricates fallback positions.

implementation_phases.title: Integrate into Plan CLI
tasks: Integrate prerequisite checks into normalize command.
Integrate index regeneration into compile command.
Optionally add resolve command for explicit identity-to-pointer lookup.
Ensure CLI output is machine-readable JSON.
acceptance_checks: normalize verifies index prerequisites and classification contract.
compile regenerates current index artifacts.
resolve returns machine-readable pointer results when implemented.

implementation_phases.title: Add validation gates
tasks: Validate unique stable identities in every index-eligible array.
Validate every emitted pointer resolves against current source JSON.
Validate source hash matches index artifact.
Detect forbidden raw array-position patches for index-eligible arrays.
Detect classification drift or stale artifacts.
acceptance_checks: Validation gate catches duplicate identities.
Validation gate catches stale index artifacts.
Validation gate catches unresolved pointers.
Validation gate catches raw positional patch usage on indexed arrays.

implementation_phases.title: Seal evidence and declare readiness
tasks: Assemble final readiness report.
Hash emitted artifacts.
Record exact output paths and pass/fail status.
acceptance_checks: All prior phases passed.
All required artifacts exist.
Final readiness report status equals PASS.

normalize_command_requirements: Canonicalize source JSON as required by current Plan CLI policy.
Verify blocker resolution for any array declared index-eligible.
Build or verify prerequisite ID maps before compile.
compile_command_requirements: Refuse success if generated artifact fails schema validation.
resolve_command_requirements: Accept file selector and semantic identity selector.
Return fail-closed error for unknown identity, ambiguous identity, or stale artifact.

required_validators.name: validate_pointer_resolution
failure_conditions: pointer resolves to unexpected node type

required_validators.name: validate_source_hash_freshness
failure_conditions: source_sha256 mismatch between artifact and current source
artifact generated from noncanonical source serialization

required_validators.name: validate_patch_mode_compliance
failure_conditions: raw numeric position patch against index-eligible array
consumer bypasses resolver or compiled index

required_validators.name: validate_classification_completeness
failure_conditions: classification conflicts with schema or observed structure

acceptance_criteria: JSON Pointer is the only authoritative exact locator in the implemented system.
Every mutable index-eligible array is accessed through semantic-id-to-pointer resolution.
No source file contains a hand-maintained inline navigation index as SSOT.
Every array in scope is explicitly classified.
All prerequisite blockers are resolved before generator implementation proceeds.
Generated artifacts are deterministic across repeated runs with unchanged input.
Plan CLI normalize and compile behaviors own verification and regeneration responsibilities.
Instruction document is either coarse-only by explicit policy or structurally refactored before fine-grained indexing.
explicit_non_goals: Do not use JSONPath as the main runtime patch locator.
Do not use byte offsets or byte-level indexing for this plan.
Do not accept array element addressing by unstable numeric position in index-eligible arrays.
Do not infer array classifications without explicit registry entries.
Do not parse markdown headings heuristically to create authoritative instruction-document index entries.
